completed_tasks.name: Transfer operator deep dive (6 variants, gauge theorem)
Explicit formula Jacobi (primes in, zeros out)

remaining_tasks.name: Find basis transformation Q between GCD and spectral spaces

human_actions_pending.context: Grok has the spec, awaiting final operator iteration

decisions.decision: Gauge symmetry theorem rules out all Hermitian transfer operators
Inert primes (3 mod 8) dominate operator coupling, split primes (5 mod 8) silent
decisions.rationale: Multi-start optimization over mod-8 residue classes

decisions.decision: Analytic continuation gives finite ||V|| = 2.69 at sigma=1/2
decisions.rationale: |zeta'(1/2)/zeta(1/2)| is finite even though term-by-term sum diverges

decisions.decision: GCD kernel and explicit formula diagonal operate on incompatible scales
decisions.rationale: Index space (1-200) has rich GCD; value space (14-400) has sparse GCD

next_action: Find the change-of-basis matrix Q between the Lanczos/multiplicative basis (where GCD works, r=0.68) and the spectral basis (where eigenvalues are correct). Q may be the discrete Mellin transform or Ramanujan sum expansion.
context_notes: The r-vs-eigenvalue-accuracy tradeoff is the central obstacle. log(k) diagonal + GCD gives r=0.68 but wrong eigenvalues. Explicit formula gives right eigenvalues but r=0.03. The basis Q that maps between them is the missing piece. Grok is actively cross-verifying and suggested the value-space GCD and gap normalization (both tested, both insufficient). The next breakthrough needs fresh mathematical thinking about Q.
